_comment: AI Character Engine — Configuration
Copy this file to engine.config.json and edit to match your setup.
RECOMMENDED: vLLM (highest throughput, 11+ decisions/sec)
Install: pip install vllm
Start:   python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model <path-or-hf-id>
The default config below is ready for vLLM on port 8100.
ALTERNATIVE: Ollama (easiest setup, 2 minutes)
Install from https://ollama.com, then: ollama pull qwen2.5:7b
See _comment_ollama_alternative below for the config swap.
OTHER PROVIDERS:
lmstudio — Load a model in the LM Studio GUI, start the local server.
openrouter / openai / anthropic — Set apiKey below.
GPU PINNING:
Use gpuId to document which GPU each provider should run on.
For vLLM, pass --gpu-id <N> when starting the server.
For Ollama, set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=<N> in the environment.
For LM Studio, select the GPU in the GUI settings.
COST WARNING:
This engine makes hundreds of LLM calls per minute when characters are active.
Cloud providers (openrouter, openai, anthropic) will incur significant costs.
Local providers (vllm, ollama, lmstudio) are strongly recommended.

inference._comment_type: One of: vllm, ollama, lmstudio, openrouter, openai, anthropic

_comment_ollama_alternative: To use Ollama instead of vLLM, replace the inference section above with:
_comment_embedding: Enable for semantic memory retrieval. Any local provider can serve embeddings.
LM Studio: load nomic-embed-text in the GUI
